title: What Are you expecting From the 20th anniversary10 04 2015
display_title: What Are you expecting From the 20th anniversary10 04 2015
wikitext:
[]: What Are you expecting From the 20th anniversary(10/04/2015) Post by Shakivara777 » Sun Sep 13, 2015 12:42 pm From October 4, 1995 to October 4, 2015 they should gift us a Ova or movie(like gainax did with diebuster/gunbuster in thier 20th anniversary)or any other Fanservice as they called it i think an evangelion anima ova its ok for me

[]: Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sun Sep 13, 2015 1:01 pm Those are pretty obscene expectations guaranteed to leave you disappointed. We already got the BD box for the 20th anniversary. If there was some animation project for release in October it would have been announced long ago. The only realistic expectations are for a bunch more merch and fan events neither which will deliver anything of substance.

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Sep 13, 2015 1:19 pm At the outside, there might be another movie airing, and the faintest chance of an "it's not dead yet" teaser for 3.0+1.0 at the very end.

[]: Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sun Sep 13, 2015 1:36 pm Ugh, the NTV Roadshow that aired all those preview just announced that they're airing american Godzilla on the 25th with Anno's Godzilla information attached to that. There's no point in winding up the Eva marketing machine when Anno is busy with the Godzilla one.

[]: Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sun Sep 13, 2015 2:52 pm All I expect to happen Eva related on the 20th Anniversary are two things: 1. A lot of fans will make crazy announcements, speculations & bitch about "Anno trolling with no Final yet". Which he isn't. Trolling is not a thing outside the world of sexist basement dwellers. 2. At best, that will be the week Animator Expo releases the short film that ties into the Evangelion universe.

[]: Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Sep 13, 2015 3:17 pm I expect the true 20th anniversary of the show will yield nothing Evangelion-related of any importance. I think it's a sad expectation to have, but given the events of the past few chronologically-significant days on the calendar, I have learned not to expect any different.

[]: Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Sep 18, 2015 7:45 am NTV Friday roadshow has just announced that on October 2nd they will be screening Howl's Moving Castle and October 9th will be When Marnie Was There. https://kinro.jointv.jp/lineup/list

[]: Post by BlueBasilisk » Fri Sep 18, 2015 8:59 am Sahaquiel will land on Khara HQ that Friday, further delaying the movie.

[]: Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Sep 18, 2015 9:49 am I'll be starting Evangelion all over again that day, watching each episode in HD in the intervals they were released in Japan 20 years ago.

[]: Post by pwhodges » Fri Sep 18, 2015 9:51 am And in preparation you'll have your memory wiped so you can replicate the experience of seeing it all for the first time?

[]: Post by Sicarius VI » Fri Sep 18, 2015 11:05 am BlueBasilisk wrote: Sahaquiel will land on Khara HQ that Friday, further delaying the movie.

[]: Post by Chuckman » Fri Sep 18, 2015 2:15 pm Anno will announce that all future Eva projects are cancelled, confirm that 3.0 will never see a US release, and stand atop a pile of burning Eva merchandise as the resulting cascade of misery draws forth all the rage and hate and lust poured into his creation over twenty years, tearing the veil between the realms of men and the realms of the unreal, and upon that pyre he shall ascend to immortal daemonhood. Also they'll put a cutesy cheesecake drawing of Rei on the website.

[]: Post by robersora » Fri Sep 18, 2015 4:01 pm ^ And all of that he will accomplish with help of his new BFF Godzilla.

[]: Post by Arcadia's legacy » Fri Sep 18, 2015 4:04 pm I think we'd all do something similar if Godzilla was the BFF for any of us

[]: Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Sep 18, 2015 6:17 pm Chuckman wrote: Anno will announce that all future Eva projects are cancelled, confirm that 3.0 will never see a US release, and stand atop a pile of burning Eva merchandise as the resulting cascade of misery draws forth all the rage and hate and lust poured into his creation over twenty years, tearing the veil between the realms of men and the realms of the unreal, and upon that pyre he shall ascend to immortal daemonhood.

[]: Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Sep 18, 2015 6:43 pm pwhodges wrote: And in preparation you'll have your memory wiped so you can replicate the experience of seeing it all for the first time?

[]: Post by Ray » Fri Sep 18, 2015 6:47 pm What am I expecting? Nothing. Nothing at all.

[]: Post by chee » Fri Sep 18, 2015 8:16 pm Hideo Kojima will fulton Anno up to their mothership and bombard the White House.

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Sep 19, 2015 12:19 am MG 1 & 2 HD remake with Anno at the helm alongside Kojima. (But seriously, probably nothing will happen. I do want to know what Anno and Kojima think of each other's works, but dunno where to research?)

[]: Post by hui43210 » Sat Sep 19, 2015 6:28 pm I expect nothing as there is no indication that I should expect something. It's a good way to avoid disappointment, hence why I have given up trying to predict when 3.0 will come out here, let alone when 3.0+1.0 will.
